title: stellaria alsine subsp. undulata
intro: Stellaria alsine subsp. undulata, also known as undulate stitchwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This perennial herb is native to Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are believed to have health benefits like soothing sore throats, reducing fever, and supporting the immune system. In addition, undulate stitchwort has been used to treat digestive issues and is said to have antimicrobial properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
